extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR Tadej attacked solo on penultimate climb with 43km to go, built 1:32 gap to take stage and jump to 3rd overall, stealing white jersey from Lopez
tactical_patterns: Astana's repeated accelerations in final 70km eliminated Roglic domestiques and reduced break advantage by 2m40s in 24km.
Pogacar attacked immediately after Lopez move failed, exploiting moment when rivals were fixated on defending Lopez rather than counterattacking.
Valverde forced to lead chase himself in final 2.5km when Pogacar threatened his 2nd place, revealing Movistar lacked climbing support.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Built 90-second gap solo on penultimate climb despite having off-day two stages prior, showing exceptional recovery pattern.
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: negative: Unable to follow or respond when Pogacar attacked, appeared fatigued in finale despite being aggressor earlier.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Jumbo controlled entire stage despite losing Kruijswijk early race and Martin previous day, revealing team depth redundancy.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Required to chase alone in finale, indicating Movistar mountain support insufficient for three-week Grand Tour podium defense.
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: neutral: Bridged 1:38 solo to break impressively but couldn't sustain when Astana ramped pace in final 70km.
